task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Shirt Color' dropdown attribute with the correct options.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Magento Admin 'Product Attributes' page showing a new attribute with Default Label 'Shirt Color', Attribute Code 'shirt_color', Catalog Input Type 'Dropdown', and options for 'White', 'Blue', and 'Black'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Shirt Color' attribute is added to the 'Default' attribute set.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Magento Admin 'Attribute Sets' page for the 'Default' set, showing the 'Shirt Color' attribute successfully dragged into one of the active attribute groups (e.g., Product Details).

task_completion.description: Verify the basic product details (Name, SKU, Price, Category) are entered correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Magento Admin product creation/edit page showing Product Name 'Oxford Dress Shirt', SKU 'SHIRT-OXFORD-001', Price '59.99', and assigned to the 'Clothing' category.

task_completion.description: Verify the product is configured to use variations based on the 'Shirt Color' attribute.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the 'Create Configurations' wizard or the Configurations section of the product page actively using the 'Shirt Color' attribute to define variations.

task_completion.description: Verify three simple product variants are generated, enabled, and have the correct quantity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the 'Configurations' grid at the bottom of the product page showing 3 generated variants (one for White, Blue, and Black). Each variant must show a Quantity of '50' and Status 'Enabled'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Magento Admin web interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser navigating the Magento Admin panel, not terminal windows executing API calls or database commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually interacted with the forms to create the items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show intermediate steps of filling out forms, clicking 'Save', and navigating menus, rather than just showing a final success screen or a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the success state.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element/Console) should be open and actively modifying the DOM to display fake success messages or product grids.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a dropdown attribute labeled 'Shirt Color' (code: 'shirt_color') with options 'White', 'Blue', and 'Black'. This attribute must be added to the 'Default' attribute set. A configurable product named 'Oxford Dress Shirt' (SKU: 'SHIRT-OXFORD-001') must be created in the 'Clothing' category with a price of $59.99. Finally, exactly 3 simple product variants (one for each color) must be generated and enabled with a quantity of 50 each.
